clusters.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days taken to hospital
clusters.neutral_headline: Indian police forcibly hospitalise activist after 20-day hunger strike
bullet_summary: Police said protesters tried to stop them from seizing Sonam Wangchuk, causing a 'slight commotion'
Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place

items.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days taken to hospital
items.standfirst: Police in India have forcibly taken an activist to hospital after a 20-day hunger strike protesting the country's examination system. The case has drawn widespread attention and renewed debate over education reforms and the handling of public protests. France 24 correspondent Navodita Kumari reports.
items.content: Police in India have forcibly taken an activist to hospital after a 20-day hunger strike protesting the country's examination system. The case has drawn widespread attention and renewed debate over education reforms and the handling of public protests. France 24 correspondent Navodita Kumari reports.

items.title: Indian police forcibly hospitalise activist after 20-day hunger strike

items.content: Police said protesters tried to stop them from seizing Sonam Wangchuk, causing a 'slight commotion'.

items.title: Hunger striker taken to hospital as police launch 'chilling crackdown'
items.standfirst: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.
items.content: Police have forcibly removed hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk from a student protest site in India.

items.title: Indian activist on hunger strike for 20 days forcibly taken to hospital
items.standfirst: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.
items.content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has now begun an indefinite fast in Sonam Wangchuk's place.

bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC World, CBS News and others

items.title: Canadian wildfire smoke shrouds US, potential risk for World Cup final
items.standfirst: Plumes of smoke from wildfires in Canada are pouring into major US cities.
items.content: Plumes of smoke from wildfires in Canada are pouring into major US cities.

items.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
items.standfirst: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.
items.content: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

items.title: Maps show wildfire smoke forecast, air quality alerts in swath of U.S.
items.standfirst: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.
items.content: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is engulfing large swaths of the Midwest and the U.S. East Coast this week.

items.title: Trump threatens Canada with higher tariffs over wildfire smoke
items.standfirst: President Trump threatened more tariffs on Canada for wildfires that have blanketed large parts of the Midwest and East Coast in smoke in recent days.
items.content: President Trump threatened more tariffs on Canada for wildfires that have blanketed large parts of the Midwest and East Coast in smoke in recent days.

items.title: Trump threatens Canada with higher tariffs over wildfire smoke
items.standfirst: President Trump on Friday called the smoke from wildfires in Canada trickling into U.S. airspace unacceptable, saying he would hold the country responsible. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.
items.content: President Trump on Friday called the smoke from wildfires in Canada trickling into U.S. airspace unacceptable, saying he would hold the country responsible. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

items.title: Trump blames Canada as US 'invaded by filthy air'
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump has blamed Canada for wildfire smoke that could impact the World Cup final.
items.content: US President Donald Trump has blamed Canada for wildfire smoke that could impact the World Cup final.

clusters.title: Zelenskyy considers sacking commander-in-chief as protests swell
clusters.neutral_headline: Dismissal of Ukraine’s defence minister highlights wider issues for Zelenskyy
bullet_summary: But he had reportedly clashed with Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, over how the war should be waged
Reported by Financial Times, France24, Guardian World

items.title: Zelenskyy considers sacking commander-in-chief as protests swell
items.standfirst: Crisis engulfs military after Ukrainian president dismissed his popular defence minister
items.content: Crisis engulfs military after Ukrainian president dismissed his popular defence minister

items.title: Ukraine: Protests over Fedorov's removal continue for a second day
items.standfirst: Protesters in Kyiv have demanded the dismissal of Ukraine's commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, as public anger continues to grow following President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's surprise cabinet reshuffle. The demonstrations entered a second day after the popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, was not reappointed. The 35-year-old has been widely credited with contributing to Ukraine's recent successes in its war with Russia, making his removal a source of significant public backlash.
items.content: Protesters in Kyiv have demanded the dismissal of Ukraine's commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, as public anger continues to grow following President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's surprise cabinet reshuffle. The demonstrations entered a second day after the popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, was not reappointed. The 35-year-old has been widely credited with contributing to Ukraine's recent successes in its war with Russia, making his removal a source of significant public backlash.

items.title: Thousands protest in Kyiv for second day over ouster of popular defence minister Fedorov
items.standfirst: Protesters rallied for a second day in the Ukrainian capital on Friday, angered by President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision to fire youthful defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen as an innovator who pioneered the country’s successful drone warfare but clashed with the traditional military establishment.
items.content: Protesters rallied for a second day in the Ukrainian capital on Friday, angered by President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision to fire youthful defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, seen as an innovator who pioneered the country’s successful drone warfare but clashed with the traditional military establishment.

items.title: Ukraine: Trouble at the Top? Adieu Keir, Alright Andy
items.standfirst: In a week that’s seen rare wartime demonstrations in Ukraine protesting the ousting of Defence Minister Mykhailo (Misha) Fedorov after six months in a job in which he’s been credited with making a difference on the battlefield and in an anti-corruption drive. But he had reportedly clashed with Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, over how the war should be waged.
items.content: In a week that’s seen rare wartime demonstrations in Ukraine protesting the ousting of Defence Minister Mykhailo (Misha) Fedorov after six months in a job in which he’s been credited with making a difference on the battlefield and in an anti-corruption drive. But he had reportedly clashed with Ukraine’s commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, over how the war should be waged.

items.title: Dismissal of Ukraine’s defence minister highlights wider issues for Zelenskyy
items.standfirst: Mykhailo Fedorov, celebrated by many for innovative, tech-driven approach, was sidelined for military old guardVolodomyr Zelenskyy’s abrupt dismissal of Ukraine’s youthful and innovative defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, at precisely the moment Kyiv appeared to be gaining advantages in several spheres of its war with Russia has exposed, not for the first time, a troubling flaw in the president’s leadership.The move, which has startled senior European officials and caused consternation, and demonstrations, in Ukraine, is all the more shocking given Fedorov’s role in pushing a clear strategy to prosecute the war, leveraging Ukraine’s rapidly developing technological advances in drone and missile technology. Continue reading...
items.content: Mykhailo Fedorov, celebrated by many for innovative, tech-driven approach, was sidelined for military old guardVolodomyr Zelenskyy’s abrupt dismissal of Ukraine’s youthful and innovative defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, at precisely the moment Kyiv appeared to be gaining advantages in several spheres of its war with Russia has exposed, not for the first time, a troubling flaw in the president’s leadership.The move, which has startled senior European officials and caused consternation, and demonstrations, in Ukraine, is all the more shocking given Fedorov’s role in pushing a clear strategy to prosecute the war, leveraging Ukraine’s rapidly developing technological advances in drone and missile technology. Continue reading...

items.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
items.standfirst: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...
items.content: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leadersUkraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership.Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challenging dialogue” between Fedorov – widely seen as a reformist and moderniser – and the military’s commander in chief, Col Gen Oleksandr Syrskyi. Continue reading...

clusters.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh night
clusters.neutral_headline: US launches seventh night of Iran strikes as Hormuz conflict escalates
bullet_summary: Kuwait said on Saturday that an Iranian attack hit a water and electricity plant one day after a similar strike damaged another plant
US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”

items.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh night
items.standfirst: Kuwait said on Saturday that an Iranian attack hit a water and electricity plant one day after a similar strike damaged another plant. Tehran launched attacks on US military sites hosted by several of its Gulf neighbours in response to a seventh night of US strikes on Iran overnight on Friday. Morgan Ayre reports.
items.content: Kuwait said on Saturday that an Iranian attack hit a water and electricity plant one day after a similar strike damaged another plant. Tehran launched attacks on US military sites hosted by several of its Gulf neighbours in response to a seventh night of US strikes on Iran overnight on Friday. Morgan Ayre reports.

items.title: US launches seventh night of Iran strikes as Hormuz conflict escalates
items.standfirst: Central Command says attacks were designed to ‘continue degrading Iranian military capabilities’The US military said it had launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran on Friday night as fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz.US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”. Continue reading...
items.content: Central Command says attacks were designed to ‘continue degrading Iranian military capabilities’The US military said it had launched a seventh consecutive night of strikes on Iran on Friday night as fighting escalated over the strait of Hormuz.US Central Command, in a post on X, said the strikes, which began at 7pm GMT, were designed to “continue degrading Iranian military capabilities”. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East live: Kuwait accuses Iran of targeting civilian sites, vital infrastructure
items.standfirst: Kuwait accused Iran on Saturday of targeting civilian sites and vital infrastructure in the country, after reporting attacks on an oil facility and a power and water plant. Tehran launched attacks on US military sites hosted by several of its Gulf neighbours in response to a seventh night of US strikes on Iran overnight on Friday. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: Kuwait accused Iran on Saturday of targeting civilian sites and vital infrastructure in the country, after reporting attacks on an oil facility and a power and water plant. Tehran launched attacks on US military sites hosted by several of its Gulf neighbours in response to a seventh night of US strikes on Iran overnight on Friday. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: France vs England: Deschamps set for final World Cup match as Zidane waits
items.standfirst: Deschamps, who led France to the 2018 World Cup title, bids farewell after Saturday's third-place playoff with England.
items.content: Deschamps, who led France to the 2018 World Cup title, bids farewell after Saturday's third-place playoff with England.

items.title: Disappointed France and England to face off in third place World Cup match
items.standfirst: France and England meet for their final World Cup match on Saturday competing for third place after failing to reach Sunday's final. Neither of the disappointed teams seems to relish the prospect of the clash: "The best thing for France and England would be for this match not to exist," said France coach Didier Deschamps.
items.content: France and England meet for their final World Cup match on Saturday competing for third place after failing to reach Sunday's final. Neither of the disappointed teams seems to relish the prospect of the clash: "The best thing for France and England would be for this match not to exist," said France coach Didier Deschamps.

items.content: The United States attacked bridges and other key infrastructure in southern Iran late Friday, capping nearly a week of strikes aimed at intensifying pressure on Tehran to give up control of the Strait of Hormuz.
